title: myosotis laxa subsp. baltica
intro: Myosotis laxa subsp. baltica, also known as Baltic forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which help to soothe respiratory issues and alleviate muscle spasms. The Baltic forget-me-not is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, providing numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, as well as potential cardiovascular and anti-cancer properties. This plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
